title: overlayfs: "Failed to resolve …: -13" on idmapped mounts
body:
### Issue Description

Privileged containers fail to start with '/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/…/mapped/…: -13' errors when using idmapped overlayfs mounts enabled.

Issue was previously reported and tracked on coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker#1305, see discussion there.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Reproducer see first few comments in coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker#1305

### Describe the results you received

Container fails to start with the following errors in the journal:

```
[    9.110800] overlayfs: POSIX ACLs are not yet supported with idmapped layers, mounting without ACL support.
[    9.435100] overlayfs: failed to resolve '/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/f2761b92efa71f4dcd11378fc58891b5fbc4b4373cac4f0eedfee2902f4c5b50/mapped/0/l/HOTNAYERIZLT6DLBLTTQHX7T2V': -13
```

Also see coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker#1305

### Describe the results you expected

Container to start

Also see coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker#1305

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No (Podman 4.5.0 didn't land in stable FCOS yet)

### Additional environment details

Persistent issue since FCOS 36.20220906.3.2 running Podman 4.2.0 on kernel 5.19.6-200.fc36.x86_64, including latest FCOS 37.20230401.3.0 running Podman 4.4.2 on kernel 6.2.8-200.fc37.x86_64 (there's no FCOS release with Podman 4.5.0 yet). Last working version was FCOS 36.20220820.3.0.

### Additional information

There are multiple known workarounds, see coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker#1305, one is to disable idmapped mounts as suggested by @giuseppe:

```console
$ mv /run/containers/storage/overlay/idmapped-lower-dir-true /run/containers/storage/overlay/idmapped-lower-dir-false
```

comments.body: please try with the latest podman release, there were many fixed for the idmapped mounts feature.
We can assume it has been fixed in podman 4.5, reopen if we are mistaken.
Just to give some feedback: Podman 4.5.0 just landed in FCOS and it was indeed fixed with Podman 4.5.0. Keep up with the great work, thank you! :heart:
